_comment: Bug report deck for a real clutter bug in the kitsoki web UI: a redundant 'Observe ↗' router-link in the InteractiveView topbar, sitting next to the Hide/show-trace toggle and easily confused with it (it navigates to the read-only observer for the same run). LIVE at tools/runstatus/src/views/InteractiveView.vue:85. The bug report carries an rrweb repro. meta.mode:pitch is required or scenes render blank. Scene shapes copied verbatim from docs/decks/agent-actions-overflow-bugfix.slidey.json and pet-prd.slidey.json.

scenes.title: A redundant Observe ↗ link
scenes.subtitle: It sits beside the trace toggle and gets confused with it
scenes.narration: A small clutter bug: a redundant Observe link in the topbar, sitting right next to the trace toggle and easily mistaken for it.

scenes.lede: Two controls that look like one job.
scenes.body: The InteractiveView topbar carries an 'Observe ↗' router-link directly next to the Hide / show-trace toggle. The toggle controls the trace column you're already looking at; the link navigates away to the read-only observer for the very same run. Same neighbourhood, near-identical affordance — so people reach for one expecting the other, click out of their live session, and have to navigate back. It's pure clutter against a control it's easily confused with.
scenes.narration: The topbar puts an Observe link right next to the trace toggle. The toggle shows or hides the trace you're already on; the link jumps to the read-only observer for the same run. They look alike, they sit together, and people click the wrong one — leaving their live session for no reason. It's clutter.

scenes.title: InteractiveView.vue · the offending markup

annotations.text: Lives in the topbar at InteractiveView.vue:85, beside the trace toggle
Navigates to the read-only observer for the same run — a dead-end relative to the live view

scenes.caption: tools/runstatus/src/views/InteractiveView.vue:85 — plus its CSS at ~937–946.
scenes.narration: Here it is: a single router-link at InteractiveView line 85, with its own CSS just below. It points at the observer route for the run you're already watching live.

scenes.title: Reproduce & impact

cards.label: Open any kitsoki web run
cards.sub: the live InteractiveView with its trace column

cards.label: Look at the topbar
cards.sub: the Observe ↗ link sits right next to the trace toggle

cards.label: Reach for the toggle, hit the link
cards.sub: you're navigated out to the read-only observer of the same run

scenes.caption: Impact: confusable controls, an accidental trip out of the live session, zero added value.
scenes.narration: To see it: open any web run, glance at the topbar, and reach for the trace toggle — the look-alike Observe link is right there to catch the misclick, sending you out to a read-only view of the run you're already on.

scenes.body: The report ships an rrweb repro — a deterministic browser replay showing the two confusable controls in the live topbar. The recommended fix is trivial and self-contained: delete the link at InteractiveView.vue:85 and its CSS rule (~937–946). No behaviour depends on it; the observer route stays reachable elsewhere. Triage recommendation: one-shot, unsupervised.
scenes.narration: The report carries an rrweb repro that replays the confusable topbar. Because the fix is a self-contained deletion with nothing depending on it, this is a clean one-shot, unsupervised candidate.
